signals.headline: Russia, China Veto UN Resolution to Reopen Strait of Hormuz | NO** | MED confidence[6]
signals.raw:
Russia, China Veto UN Resolution to Reopen Strait of Hormuz | NO** | MED confidence[6]

Russia and China vetoed a UN Security Council resolution aimed at reopening the Strait of Hormuz, blocking international efforts to ease tensions amid the ongoing Iran conflict and ceasefire strains[6]. signals.raw:
BANK OF KOREA HOLDS RATE AT 2.5% AMID MIDDLE EAST INFLATION CONCERNS | NO rate cut | HIGH confidence**

The Bank of Korea's Monetary Policy Board unanimously decided on April 10, 2026, to maintain its benchmark interest rate at 2.5% for the seventh consecutive month.[3] This decision comes as **inflation has climbed above the central bank's 2% target**, with recent CPI inflation reported at approx

signals.raw: The **weakening Korean won** (around 1,520 won per U.S. dollar) is amplifying import cost pressures.[3]

signals.headline: The **Middle East conflict** is adding uncertainty to the economic outlook, offsetting any consideration for rate cuts
signals.raw:
The **Middle East conflict** is adding uncertainty to the economic outlook, offsetting any consideration for rate cuts.[3]

This announcement signals that **rate cuts remain off the table** despite moderating headline inflation in recent months, as policymakers prioritize managing inflation risks and currency volatility over monetary easing.
